evidence_quotes: frontend/src/utils.ts:25-26 — `if (available === 0) { return \`All copies on loan — ${queueDepth} waiting\`; } return \`${available} of ${total} available\`;` (thoughtful microcopy)

frontend/src/pages/MemberProfilePage.tsx:152-155 — destructive confirm modal uses default primary green button: `<button onClick={() => void confirm()} type="button">Confirm</button>` (no distinct danger treatment)
frontend/src/pages/BooksPage.tsx:57-59 — only secondary affordance on the catalog is `<RefreshCw …/> Refresh`; no sort or filter is wired
frontend/src/utils.ts:16-22 — live HH:MM:SS countdown via `formatCountdown` and MemberProfilePage.tsx:43-49 `setInterval(...,1000)` updating notified reservation
parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Anchor 4 ('Polished') requires multiple specific bullets that are not satisfied: (a) overdue loans / unpaid fines are not visually emphasized with color+icon — overdue is just the text '· overdue' (MemberProfilePage.tsx:101) and the unpaid pill uses color only; (b) the confirm-destructive modal uses the default primary green confirm button (MemberProfilePage.tsx:154), not a distinct danger treatment; (c) the catalog has no sort or filter affordance — BooksPage.tsx exposes only a Refresh button. The live HH:MM:SS countdown and the 'All copies on loan — N waiting' microcopy match anchor 4 examples, but the unmet bullets prevent the score; per the rubric note 'Score what is there, not what could be there', so this lands at 3.
